goal_title_suggestions: Drop the weight and keep it off
Get lean through consistent daily action

variants: Set up MyFitnessPal. Download the app and create your free account now.
Get MyFitnessPal ready. Install it and sign up in 2 minutes.
Start tracking food. Get MyFitnessPal on your phone and make an account.
tasks.why: This app will be your daily food log for the next 90 days.

variants: Check your weight. Step on the scale and write down today's number.
Log your start weight. Weigh yourself and note the number in your phone.
Find your baseline. Step on the scale and save your weight somewhere.
tasks.why: You need a starting number so you can see real progress later.

variants: Take a progress photo. Snap a mirror selfie and save it.
Start your photo record. Take a front-facing photo of yourself right now.
Capture day one. Take a quick mirror photo and keep it safe.
tasks.why: A before photo is proof of how far you'll come.

variants: Track your food. Log breakfast in MyFitnessPal right after you eat.
Log your breakfast. Open MyFitnessPal and add everything you had.
Record your morning meal. Type your breakfast into MyFitnessPal now.
tasks.why: Logging one meal a day is how the habit starts.

variants: Drink more water. Finish 1 full glass of water right now.
Hydrate today. Pour a glass of water and drink it before you move on.
Hit your water. Drink one glass of water right now.
tasks.why: Staying hydrated helps your body function and feel full.

variants: Move your body. Walk outside for 5 minutes right now.
Take a short walk. Step outside and walk for 5 minutes.
Get moving. Go for a quick 5-minute walk today.
tasks.why: A 5-minute walk is enough to start building the movement habit.

variants: Track your lunch. Log what you ate in MyFitnessPal after your midday meal.
Log lunch today. Open MyFitnessPal and add everything you had at lunch.
Record your noon meal. Type today's lunch into MyFitnessPal.
tasks.why: Tracking lunch gives you a fuller picture of your day.

variants: Drink more water. Have 2 glasses of water before dinner tonight.
Pre-dinner hydration. Drink a glass of water now and one more before dinner.
Up your water. Have two glasses of water before your evening meal.
tasks.why: Drinking water before meals naturally helps you eat less.

variants: Move your body. Walk for 5 minutes after dinner tonight.
Post-dinner walk. Take a quick 5-minute walk after you eat.
Walk it off. Go for a short walk right after dinner.
tasks.why: Walking after eating helps digestion and burns a few extra calories.

variants: Track two meals. Log breakfast and lunch in MyFitnessPal today.
Double your logging. Add both breakfast and lunch to MyFitnessPal.
Log two today. Open MyFitnessPal and enter breakfast and lunch.
tasks.why: Tracking two meals shows you where most of your food comes from.

variants: Swap a snack. Eat an apple or a handful of nuts instead of chips today.
Pick a better snack. Choose fruit or nuts over candy or chips right now.
Make a small swap. Trade one snack today for a piece of fruit.
tasks.why: One swap at a time is how eating habits change.

variants: Move your body. Take a 5-minute walk at any point today.
Short walk today. Go for 5 minutes outside whenever you can.
Get outside. Walk for 5 minutes sometime today.
tasks.why: Small daily movement adds up over weeks and months.

variants: Track your dinner. Log your evening meal in MyFitnessPal tonight.
Log dinner tonight. Open MyFitnessPal and add everything you ate.
Record your dinner. Type tonight's meal into MyFitnessPal.
tasks.why: Dinner is often where people underestimate how much they eat.

variants: Drink more water. Fill a large bottle and finish it before bed tonight.
Hit your water goal. Drink 3 glasses of water spread through today.
Stay hydrated. Drink a glass of water now and two more today.
tasks.why: Most people are mildly dehydrated and don't know it.

variants: Move your body. Walk for 5 minutes before you sit down for the evening.
End-of-day walk. Take a quick 5-minute walk before winding down.
Evening stroll. Go outside for a 5-minute walk tonight.
tasks.why: Moving in the evening helps your body recover and sleep better.

variants: Track all three meals. Log breakfast, lunch, and dinner in MyFitnessPal today.
Full day log. Add every meal to MyFitnessPal before you sleep tonight.
Log the whole day. Open MyFitnessPal and enter all three meals.
tasks.why: Seeing a full day helps you spot where extra calories come from.

variants: Swap a drink. Have water instead of soda or juice at one meal today.
Skip the sugary drink. Choose water over soda or juice right now.
Cut a liquid calorie. Drink water instead of juice or soda at lunch.
tasks.why: Liquid calories are an easy place to cut without feeling hungry.

variants: Move your body. Walk for 5 minutes outside today.
Short walk today. Get outside for a quick 5-minute walk.
Take a walk. Step outside and walk for 5 minutes anytime today.
tasks.why: Consistency with short walks builds a lasting movement habit.

variants: Review your week. Count how many meals you logged in MyFitnessPal this week.
Weekly food check. Open MyFitnessPal and look at what you logged this week.
Check your log. Scroll through MyFitnessPal and see your meals from the past 7 days.
tasks.why: Reviewing the week shows your progress and where to improve.

variants: Check your weight. Step on the scale and write down today's number.
Weekly weigh-in. Weigh yourself this morning and log the number.
Log your weight. Step on the scale and note the number in your phone.
tasks.why: Weighing in weekly tracks your real progress over time.

variants: Take a progress photo. Snap a mirror selfie and save it next to day 1.
Week-one photo. Take a front-facing photo and compare it to your start.
Capture week one. Take a quick mirror photo today.
tasks.why: Photos show changes the scale doesn't always capture.

variants: Track your breakfast. Log your morning meal in MyFitnessPal right after eating.
Log breakfast now. Open MyFitnessPal and add what you had this morning.
Start your food log. Add today's breakfast to MyFitnessPal.
tasks.why: Logging breakfast sets up a full day of tracking.

variants: Drink more water. Drink a glass of water before every meal today.
Pre-meal water habit. Have a glass of water before breakfast, lunch, and dinner.
Water before meals. Drink one glass of water before each meal today.
tasks.why: Drinking water before eating helps you feel full sooner.

variants: Move your body. Walk for 5 minutes outside today.
Short walk today. Step outside for a 5-minute walk at any point.
Take a walk. Go outside and walk for 5 minutes.
tasks.why: Daily walks are one of the most effective habits for weight loss.

variants: Track your lunch. Log your midday meal in MyFitnessPal today.
Log lunch now. Open MyFitnessPal and enter what you ate for lunch.
Add lunch to your log. Type today's lunch into MyFitnessPal.
tasks.why: Consistent logging is the single best thing you can do today.

variants: Swap a snack. Replace chips or cookies with fruit or nuts right now.
Pick a better snack. Grab an apple or nuts instead of something processed.
Make a smart swap. Trade your usual snack for fruit or a handful of nuts.
tasks.why: Small snack swaps reduce hundreds of calories without much effort.

variants: Move your body. Walk for 5 minutes outside today.
Short walk today. Take a 5-minute walk at any point.
Get outside. Walk for 5 minutes sometime today.

tasks.why: Dinner is often the biggest meal — logging it keeps you honest.

variants: Drink more water. Finish 3 glasses of water throughout today.
